render.h(3) Library Functions Manual render.h(3)

NAME

render.h - Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define VIDEO_RENDER_FPS 60
#define AUDIO_RENDER_FPS 100

Functions


void * client_video_render_thread (void *arg)
Interruptible sleep function with platform-specific optimizations. void * client_audio_render_thread (void *arg)
Main audio rendering thread function for individual clients. int create_client_render_threads (server_context_t *server_ctx, client_info_t *client)
Create and initialize per-client rendering threads. void stop_client_render_threads (client_info_t *client)
Stop and cleanup per-client rendering threads.

Detailed Description

Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting.

Definition in file server/render.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define AUDIO_RENDER_FPS 100

Definition at line 26 of file server/render.h.

Referenced by client_audio_render_thread().

#define VIDEO_RENDER_FPS 60

Definition at line 22 of file server/render.h.

Referenced by client_video_render_thread().

Function Documentation

void * client_audio_render_thread (void * arg)

Main audio rendering thread function for individual clients. This is the audio processing thread that generates personalized audio mixes for a specific client at 100fps (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz). Each client receives audio from all other clients while excluding their own audio to prevent echo and feedback.

THREAD EXECUTION FLOW:

1.
INITIALIZATION:
•
Validate client parameters and socket
•
Allocate local mixing buffer
•
Log thread startup for debugging
2.
MAIN PROCESSING LOOP:
•
Check thread running state (with mutex protection)
•
Take atomic snapshot of client state
•
Generate audio mix excluding this client's audio
•
Queue mixed audio for delivery to client
•
Sleep for precise timing (10ms intervals)
3.
CLEANUP AND EXIT:
•
Log thread termination
•
Return NULL to indicate clean exit

AUDIO MIXING STRATEGY:

ECHO PREVENTION:

  • Excludes client's own audio from their mix
  • Prevents audio feedback loops
  • Uses mixer_process_excluding_source() function
  • Maintains audio quality for other participants

PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:

TIMING PRECISION:

  • Target: 100fps (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
  • Accumulates to 960 samples (20ms) for Opus encoding
  • Fixed timing (no dynamic rate adjustment)
  • Low-latency audio delivery

BUFFER MANAGEMENT:

  • Uses fixed-size local buffer (AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER)
  • No dynamic allocation in processing loop
  • Predictable memory usage
  • Cache-friendly processing pattern

THREAD SAFETY MECHANISMS:

STATE SYNCHRONIZATION:

  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for state access
  • Implements snapshot pattern for client ID and queues
  • Prevents race conditions during client disconnect
  • Safe concurrent access with other threads

MIXER COORDINATION:

  • Global audio mixer is internally thread-safe
  • Multiple audio threads can process concurrently
  • Lock-free audio buffer operations where possible

AUDIO PIPELINE INTEGRATION:

AUDIO MIXING:

  • Uses global g_audio_mixer for multi-client processing
  • Processes AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER samples per iteration
  • Handles varying number of active audio sources

AUDIO DELIVERY:

  • Queues audio directly in client's audio packet queue
  • Higher priority than video packets in send thread
  • Real-time delivery requirements

ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY:

  • Invalid client parameters: Log error and exit immediately
  • Missing audio mixer: Continue with polling (mixer may initialize later)
  • Queue failures: Log debug info and continue (expected under load)
  • Client disconnect: Clean exit without error spam

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:

  • Local buffer allocation (no malloc in loop)
  • Minimal processing per iteration
  • Efficient mixer integration
  • Low-overhead packet queuing

Parameters

arg Pointer to client_info_t for the target client

Returns

NULL on thread completion (always clean exit)

Note

This function runs in its own dedicated thread

Audio processing has higher priority than video

Thread lifetime matches client connection lifetime

Warning

Invalid client parameter causes immediate thread termination

Thread must be joined properly to prevent resource leaks

See also

mixer_process_excluding_source() For Audio mixing implementation

AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER For buffer size constant

Definition at line 765 of file server/render.c.

765                                             {
766   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
767 
768   if (!client) {
769     log_error("Invalid client info in audio render thread");
770     return NULL;
771   }
772 
773   // Take snapshot of client ID and display name at start to avoid race conditions
774   char thread_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
775   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_client_id, client->client_id, sizeof(thread_client_id) - 1);
776   char thread_display_name[64];
777   bool is_webrtc = (client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
778   (void)is_webrtc; // May be unused in release builds
779 
780   // LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Only need client_state_mutex, not global rwlock
781   // We already have a stable client pointer
782   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
783   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_display_name, client->display_name, sizeof(thread_display_name));
784   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
785 
786 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
787   log_debug("Audio render thread started for client %u (%s), webrtc=%d", thread_client_id, thread_display_name,
788             is_webrtc);
789 #endif
790 
791   // Mix buffer: up to 960 samples for adaptive reading
792   // Normal: 480 samples = 10ms @ 48kHz
793   // Catchup: 960 samples = 20ms when buffers are filling up
794   float mix_buffer[960];
795 
796 // Opus frame accumulation buffer (960 samples = 20ms @ 48kHz)
797 // Opus requires minimum 480 samples, 960 is optimal for 20ms frames
798 #define OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES 960
799   float opus_frame_buffer[OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES];
800   int opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
801 
802   // Create Opus encoder for this client's audio stream (48kHz, mono, 128kbps, AUDIO mode for music quality)
803   opus_codec_t *opus_encoder = opus_codec_create_encoder(OPUS_APPLICATION_AUDIO, 48000, 128000);
804   if (!opus_encoder) {
805     log_error("Failed to create Opus encoder for audio render thread (client %u)", thread_client_id);
806     return NULL;
807   }
808 
809   // FPS tracking for audio render thread
810   fps_t audio_fps_tracker = {0};
811   fps_init(&audio_fps_tracker, AUDIO_RENDER_FPS, "SERVER AUDIO");
812 
813   // Adaptive sleep for audio rate limiting at 100 FPS (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
814   adaptive_sleep_state_t audio_sleep_state = {0};
815   adaptive_sleep_config_t audio_config = {
816       .baseline_sleep_ns = 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, // 10ms = 100 FPS (480 samples @ 48kHz)
817       .min_speed_multiplier = 1.0,             // Constant rate (no slowdown)
818       .max_speed_multiplier = 1.0,             // Constant rate (no speedup)
819       .speedup_rate = 0.0,                     // No adaptive behavior (constant rate)
820       .slowdown_rate = 0.0                     // No adaptive behavior (constant rate)
821   };
822   adaptive_sleep_init(&audio_sleep_state, &audio_config);
823 
824   // Per-thread counters (NOT static - each thread instance gets its own)
825   int backpressure_check_counter = 0;
826   int server_audio_frame_count = 0;
827 
828   bool should_continue = true;
829   while (should_continue && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
830     log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Audio render loop iteration for client %u", thread_client_id);
831 
832     // Check for immediate shutdown
833     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
834       log_debug("Audio render thread stopping for client %u (g_should_exit)", thread_client_id);
835       break;
836     }
837 
838     // Check thread state before acquiring any locks to prevent use-after-destroy.
839     // If we acquire locks after client is being destroyed, we'll crash with SIGSEGV
840     should_continue = (((int)atomic_load_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running) != 0) &&
841                        ((int)atomic_load_bool(&client->active) != 0) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down));
842 
843     if (!should_continue) {
844       log_debug("Audio render thread stopping for client %u (should_continue=false)", thread_client_id);
845       break;
846     }
847 
848     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
849       log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Audio render waiting for mixer (client %u)", thread_client_id);
850       // Check shutdown flag while waiting
851       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit))
852         break;
853       platform_sleep_ns(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
854       continue;
855     }
856 
857     // Optimization: No mutex needed - all fields are atomic or stable.
858     // client_id: atomic_t - use atomic_load for thread safety
859     // active: atomic_t - use atomic_load
860     // audio_queue: Assigned once at init and never changes
861     const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;         // Atomic read
862     bool active_snapshot = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);        // Atomic read
863     packet_queue_t *audio_queue_snapshot = client->audio_queue; // Stable after init
864 
865     // Check if client is still active after getting snapshot
866     if (!active_snapshot || !audio_queue_snapshot) {
867       break;
868     }
869 
870     // Create mix excluding THIS client's audio using snapshot data
871     START_TIMER("mix_%s", client_id_snapshot);
872 
873     // ADAPTIVE READING: Read more samples when we're behind to catch up
874     // Normal: 480 samples per 10ms iteration
875     // When behind: read up to 960 samples to catch up faster
876     // Check source buffer levels to decide
877     int samples_to_read = 480; // Default: 10ms worth
878 
879     // Log latency at each stage in the server pipeline
880     // NOTE: Skipping mixer source latency logging due to potential use-after-free
881     // when source IDs are freed during concurrent client cleanup
882     if (g_audio_mixer) {
883       // Log outgoing queue latency
884       size_t queue_depth = packet_queue_size(audio_queue_snapshot);
885       float queue_latency_ms = (float)queue_depth * 20.0f; // ~20ms per Opus packet
886       log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "LATENCY: Server send queue for client %s: %.1fms (%zu packets)",
887                     client_id_snapshot, queue_latency_ms, queue_depth);
888     }
889 
890     int samples_mixed = 0;
891     if (GET_OPTION(no_audio_mixer)) {
892       // Disable mixer.h processing: simple mixing without ducking/compression/etc
893       // Just add audio from all sources except this client, no processing
894       SAFE_MEMSET(mix_buffer, samples_to_read * sizeof(float), 0, samples_to_read * sizeof(float));
895 
896       if (g_audio_mixer) {
897         int max_samples_in_frame = 0;
898         // Simple mixing: just add all sources except current client
899         for (int i = 0; i < g_audio_mixer->max_sources; i++) {
900           if (g_audio_mixer->source_ids[i] && strcmp(g_audio_mixer->source_ids[i], client_id_snapshot) != 0 &&
901               g_audio_mixer->source_buffers[i]) {
902             // Read from this source and add to mix buffer
903             float temp_buffer[960]; // Max adaptive read size
904             int samples_read =
905                 (int)audio_ring_buffer_read(g_audio_mixer->source_buffers[i], temp_buffer, samples_to_read);
906 
907             // Track the maximum samples we got from any source
908             if (samples_read > max_samples_in_frame) {
909               max_samples_in_frame = samples_read;
910             }
911 
912             // Add to mix buffer
913             for (int j = 0; j < samples_read; j++) {
914               mix_buffer[j] += temp_buffer[j];
915             }
916           }
917         }
918         samples_mixed = max_samples_in_frame; // Only count samples we actually read
919       }
920 
921       log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
922                       "Audio mixer DISABLED (--no-audio-mixer): simple mixing, samples=%d for client %u", samples_mixed,
923                       client_id_snapshot);
924     } else {
925       // Use adaptive sample count in normal mixer mode
926       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client_id_snapshot);
927       samples_mixed = mixer_process_excluding_source(g_audio_mixer, mix_buffer, samples_to_read, client_id_hash);
928     }
929 
930     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "mix_%s", "Mixer for client %s: took",
931                              client_id_snapshot);
932 
933     // Debug logging every 100 iterations (disabled - can slow down audio rendering)
934     // log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Audio render for client %u: samples_mixed=%d", client_id_snapshot,
935     // samples_mixed);
936 
937     // Accumulate all samples (including 0 or partial) until we have a full Opus frame
938     // This maintains continuous stream without silence padding
939     START_TIMER("accum_%s", client_id_snapshot);
940 
941     int space_available = OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES - opus_frame_accumulated;
942     int samples_to_copy = (samples_mixed <= space_available) ? samples_mixed : space_available;
943 
944     // Only copy if we have samples, otherwise just wait for next frame
945     if (samples_to_copy > 0) {
946       SAFE_MEMCPY(opus_frame_buffer + opus_frame_accumulated,
947                   (OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES - opus_frame_accumulated) * sizeof(float), mix_buffer,
948                   samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
949       opus_frame_accumulated += samples_to_copy;
950     }
951 
952     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 2 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "accum_%s", "Accumulate for client %s: took",
953                              client_id_snapshot);
954 
955     // Only encode and send when we have accumulated a full Opus frame
956     if (opus_frame_accumulated >= OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES) {
957       // OPTIMIZATION: Don't check queue depth every iteration - it's expensive (requires lock)
958       // Only check periodically every 100 iterations (~0.6s at 172 fps)
959       // NOTE: backpressure_check_counter is now per-thread (not static), so each client thread has its own counter
960       bool apply_backpressure = false;
961 
962       if (++backpressure_check_counter >= 100) {
963         backpressure_check_counter = 0;
964         size_t queue_depth = packet_queue_size(audio_queue_snapshot);
965         // Opus frames are produced at ~50 FPS (20ms each)
966         // Reduced threshold from 50 to 10 packets (~200ms buffer) to prevent audio gaps
967         // On localhost, queue shouldn't back up at all; aggressive thresholds cause frame skipping
968         apply_backpressure = (queue_depth > 10); // > 10 packets = ~200ms buffered at 50 FPS
969 
970         if (apply_backpressure) {
971           log_warn_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
972                          "Audio backpressure for client %s: queue depth %zu packets (%.1fms buffered)",
973                          client_id_snapshot, queue_depth, (float)queue_depth / 50.0f * 1000.0f);
974         }
975       }
976 
977       if (apply_backpressure) {
978         // Skip this packet to let the queue drain
979         // Reset accumulation buffer so fresh samples can be captured on next iteration.
980         // Without this reset, we'd loop forever with stale audio and no space for new samples
981         opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
982         // Reduced sleep from 5.8ms to 1ms to minimize audio gaps on localhost
983         platform_sleep_ns(1 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
984         continue;
985       }
986 
987       // Encode accumulated Opus frame (960 samples = 20ms @ 48kHz)
988       uint8_t opus_buffer[1024]; // Max Opus frame size
989 
990       START_TIMER("opus_encode_%s", client_id_snapshot);
991 
992       int opus_size =
993           opus_codec_encode(opus_encoder, opus_frame_buffer, OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES, opus_buffer, sizeof(opus_buffer));
994 
995       STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "opus_encode_%s",
996                                "Opus encode for client %s: took", client_id_snapshot);
997 
998       // DEBUG: Log mix buffer and encoding results to see audio levels being sent
999       {
1000         float peak = 0.0f, rms = 0.0f;
1001         for (int i = 0; i < OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES; i++) {
1002           float abs_val = fabsf(opus_frame_buffer[i]);
1003           if (abs_val > peak)
1004             peak = abs_val;
1005           rms += opus_frame_buffer[i] * opus_frame_buffer[i];
1006         }
1007         rms = sqrtf(rms / OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES);
1008         // NOTE: server_audio_frame_count is now per-thread (not static), so each client thread has its own counter
1009         server_audio_frame_count++;
1010         if (server_audio_frame_count <= 5 || server_audio_frame_count % 20 == 0) {
1011           // Log first 4 samples to verify they look like valid audio (not NaN/Inf/garbage)
1012           log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
1013                         "Server audio frame #%d for client %u: samples_mixed=%d, Peak=%.6f, RMS=%.6f, opus_size=%d, "
1014                         "first4=[%.4f,%.4f,%.4f,%.4f]",
1015                         server_audio_frame_count, client_id_snapshot, samples_mixed, peak, rms, opus_size,
1016                         opus_frame_buffer[0], opus_frame_buffer[1], opus_frame_buffer[2], opus_frame_buffer[3]);
1017         }
1018       }
1019 
1020       // Always reset accumulation buffer after attempting to encode - we've consumed these samples
1021       // If we don't reset, new audio samples would be dropped while stale data sits in the buffer
1022       opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
1023 
1024       if (opus_size <= 0) {
1025         log_error("Failed to encode audio to Opus for client %s: opus_size=%d", client_id_snapshot, opus_size);
1026       } else {
1027         // Wrap Opus data in proper batch packet format for client parsing:
1028         // Header: sample_rate (4B) + frame_duration (4B) + frame_count (4B)
1029         // Frame sizes: frame_count * 2B (one uint16 per frame with Opus data size)
1030         // Opus data: raw encoded audio
1031 
1032         // Fixed values for Opus encoding (standard 48kHz, 960 samples per frame = 20ms)
1033         const uint32_t sample_rate = 48000;
1034         const uint32_t frame_duration = 20; // milliseconds (960 samples / 48kHz = 20ms)
1035         const uint32_t frame_count = 1;     // one Opus frame per packet
1036         const uint16_t frame_size = (uint16_t)opus_size;
1037 
1038         // Calculate total packet size: 16-byte header + frame_sizes + opus data
1039         const size_t header_size = 16; // 4 uint32s (sample_rate, frame_duration, frame_count, reserved)
1040         const size_t frame_sizes_size = (size_t)frame_count * sizeof(uint16_t);
1041         const size_t total_packet_size = header_size + frame_sizes_size + (size_t)opus_size;
1042 
1043         // Allocate buffer for the complete packet
1044         uint8_t *packet_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(total_packet_size, uint8_t *);
1045         if (!packet_buffer) {
1046           log_error("Failed to allocate Opus batch packet buffer for client %s (size=%zu)", client_id_snapshot,
1047                     total_packet_size);
1048         } else {
1049           // Build header in network byte order
1050           uint8_t *ptr = packet_buffer;
1051 
1052           // Write sample_rate (4 bytes, network byte order)
1053           uint32_t sr_net = htonl(sample_rate);
1054           memcpy(ptr, &sr_net, 4);
1055           ptr += 4;
1056 
1057           // Write frame_duration (4 bytes, network byte order)
1058           uint32_t fd_net = htonl(frame_duration);
1059           memcpy(ptr, &fd_net, 4);
1060           ptr += 4;
1061 
1062           // Write frame_count (4 bytes, network byte order)
1063           uint32_t fc_net = htonl(frame_count);
1064           memcpy(ptr, &fc_net, 4);
1065           ptr += 4;
1066 
1067           // Write reserved field (4 bytes, all zeros)
1068           uint32_t reserved = 0;
1069           memcpy(ptr, &reserved, 4);
1070           ptr += 4;
1071 
1072           // Write frame sizes (2 bytes per frame, network byte order)
1073           for (uint32_t i = 0; i < frame_count; i++) {
1074             uint16_t fs_net = htons(frame_size);
1075             memcpy(ptr, &fs_net, 2);
1076             ptr += 2;
1077           }
1078 
1079           // Copy Opus data
1080           memcpy(ptr, opus_buffer, (size_t)opus_size);
1081 
1082           // Queue the properly formatted packet
1083           START_TIMER("audio_queue_%s", client_id_snapshot);
1084 
1085           int result = packet_queue_enqueue(audio_queue_snapshot, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_buffer,
1086                                             total_packet_size, 0, true);
1087 
1088           STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "audio_queue_%s",
1089                                    "Audio queue for client %s: took", client_id_snapshot);
1090 
1091           if (result < 0) {
1092             log_debug("Failed to queue Opus audio for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
1093           } else {
1094             // FPS tracking - audio packet successfully queued (handles lag detection and periodic reporting)
1095             fps_frame_ns(&audio_fps_tracker, time_get_ns(), "audio packet queued");
1096           }
1097 
1098           // Free the allocated packet buffer after queuing (packet_queue_enqueue copies data)
1099           SAFE_FREE(packet_buffer);
1100         }
1101       }
1102       // NOTE: opus_frame_accumulated is already reset at line 928 after encode attempt
1103     }
1104 
1105     // Audio mixing rate limiting using adaptive sleep system
1106     // Target: 10ms intervals (100 FPS) for 480 samples @ 48kHz
1107     // Use queue_depth=0 and target_depth=0 for constant-rate audio processing
1108     adaptive_sleep_do(&audio_sleep_state, 0, 0);
1109   }
1110 
1111 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1112   log_debug("Audio render thread stopped for client %u", thread_client_id);
1113 #endif
1114 
1115   // Clean up Opus encoder
1116   if (opus_encoder) {
1117     opus_codec_destroy(opus_encoder);
1118   }
1119 
1120   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
1121   asciichat_errno_destroy();
1122 
1123   return NULL;
1124 }




References client_info::active, adaptive_sleep_do(),
    adaptive_sleep_init(), asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::audio_queue,
    AUDIO_RENDER_FPS, client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    audio_ring_buffer_read(), client_info::client_id,
    client_info::client_state_mutex, client_info::display_name,
    fps_frame_ns(), fps_init(), g_audio_mixer,
    g_should_exit, GET_OPTION, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    log_error, LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, LOG_RATE_SLOW,
    log_warn_every, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN,
    mixer_process_excluding_source(), mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT,
    OPUS_APPLICATION_AUDIO, opus_codec_create_encoder(),
    opus_codec_destroy(), opus_codec_encode(),
    OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES, packet_queue_enqueue(),
    packet_queue_size(), PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH,
    platform_sleep_ns(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_MEMCPY, SAFE_MEMSET, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    client_info::shutting_down, client_info::socket,
    START_TIMER, STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by create_client_render_threads().




void
  * client_video_render_thread (void * arg)


Interruptible sleep function with platform-specific optimizations.
    Provides a sleep mechanism that can be interrupted by the global shutdown
    signal, enabling responsive thread termination. The implementation varies by
    platform to optimize for responsiveness vs CPU usage.










PLATFORM-SPECIFIC
  BEHAVIOR: 


WINDOWS IMPLEMENTATION:


    
  
  • Uses Sleep() API with millisecond precision
    • 
  
  • Simple approach due to Windows timer characteristics
    • 
  
  • Sleep(1) may sleep up to 15.6ms due to timer resolution
    • 
  
  • Checks shutdown flag before and after sleep
    • 



POSIX IMPLEMENTATION (Linux/macOS):


    
  
  • Uses condition variables for precise timing
    • 
  
  • Can be interrupted immediately by shutdown signal
    • 
  
  • Higher responsiveness for thread termination
    • 
  
  • Uses static mutex/condition variable for coordination
    • 







USAGE
  PATTERNS: 


    
  
  • Rate limiting in render threads (maintain target FPS)
    • 
  
  • Backoff delays when queues are full
    • 
  
  • Polling intervals for state changes
    • 
  
  • Graceful busy-wait alternatives
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Low CPU overhead (proper sleep, not busy wait)
    • 
  
  • Sub-second responsiveness to shutdown
    • 
  
  • Maintains timing precision for media processing
    • 
  
  • Scales well with many concurrent threads
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Early return if shutdown flag is set
    • 
  
  • Platform-specific error handling for sleep APIs
    • 
  
  • Logging for debugging (throttled to avoid spam)
    • 



Parameters


usec Sleep duration in microseconds


Note


Function may return early if shutdown is requested



Minimum sleep time is platform-dependent (typically 1ms)




Static variables are used for call counting (debugging)




Warning


Not suitable for precise timing (use for rate limiting
  only)


See also


VIDEO_RENDER_FPS For Video thread timing
  requirements


Main video rendering thread function for individual clients




This is the core video processing thread that generates
    personalized ASCII art frames for a specific client at 60fps. Each connected
    client gets their own dedicated video thread, providing linear performance
    scaling and personalized rendering based on terminal capabilities.








THREAD EXECUTION FLOW: 



  
1.

  
INITIALIZATION:

  
 •

  
Validate client parameters and socket

  
 •

  
Initialize timing variables for rate limiting

  
 •

  
Log thread startup for debugging

  
2.

  
MAIN PROCESSING LOOP:

  
 •

  
Check thread running state (with mutex protection)

  
 •

  
Rate limit to 60fps using high-resolution timing

  
 •

  
Take atomic snapshot of client state

  
 •

  
Generate personalized ASCII frame for this client

  
 •

  
Queue frame for delivery to client

  
 •

  
Update timing for next iteration

  
3.

  
CLEANUP AND EXIT:

  
 •

  
Log thread termination

  
 •

  
Return NULL to indicate clean exit








PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS: 


TIMING PRECISION:


    
  
  • Target: 60fps (16.67ms intervals)
    • 
  
  • Uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC for accurate timing
    • 
  
  • Only sleeps if ahead of schedule (no CPU waste)
    • 
  
  • Maintains consistent frame rate under varying load
    • 



CLIENT-SPECIFIC PROCESSING:


    
  
  • Generates frames customized for client's terminal
    • 
  
  • Respects color depth, palette, and size preferences
    • 
  
  • Adapts to client capability changes dynamically
    • 
  
  • Handles client disconnection gracefully
    • 







THREAD SAFETY MECHANISMS: 


STATE SYNCHRONIZATION:


    
  
  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for all state access
    • 
  
  • Implements snapshot pattern (copy state, release lock)
    • 
  
  • Prevents race conditions with other threads
    • 
  
  • Safe concurrent access to client data
    • 



SHUTDOWN COORDINATION:


    
  
  • Monitors global g_should_exit flag
    • 
  
  • Checks per-client running flags with mutex protection
    • 
  
  • Responds to shutdown within one frame interval (16.67ms)
    • 







VIDEO
  PIPELINE INTEGRATION: 


FRAME GENERATION:


    
  
  • Calls create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() from
    stream.c
    • 
  
  • Provides client-specific dimensions and preferences
    • 
  
  • Handles frame generation failures gracefully
    • 



FRAME DELIVERY:


    
  
  • Calls queue_ascii_frame_for_client() for packet queuing
    • 
  
  • Uses client's dedicated video packet queue
    • 
  
  • Send thread delivers frames asynchronously
    • 







ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY: 


    
  
  • Invalid client parameters: Log error and exit immediately
    • 
  
  • Frame generation failures: Continue with debug logging
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow: Continue with debug logging (expected under load)
    • 
  
  • Client disconnect: Clean exit without error spam
    • 
  
  • Timing issues: Self-correcting (no accumulation)
    • 







LOGGING
  AND MONITORING: 


    
  
  • Success logging: Throttled (every ~4 seconds)
    • 
  
  • Failure logging: Throttled (every ~10 seconds)
    • 
  
  • Performance metrics: Frame count and size tracking
    • 
  
  • Debug information: Timing and queue status
    • 



Parameters


arg Pointer to client_info_t for the target
  client


Returns


NULL on thread completion (always clean exit)


Note


This function runs in its own dedicated thread



Thread lifetime matches client connection lifetime




Memory allocated for frames is automatically freed




Warning


Invalid client parameter causes immediate thread
  termination



Thread must be joined properly to prevent resource leaks




See also


create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() For
  frame generation



queue_ascii_frame_for_client() For frame delivery




VIDEO_RENDER_FPS For timing constant definition




Definition at line 340 of file server/render.c.


340                                             {
341   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
342   if (!client) {
343     log_error("NULL client pointer in video render thread");
344     return NULL;
345   }
346 
347   // Take snapshot of client ID and socket at start to avoid race conditions
348   char thread_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
349   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_client_id, client->client_id, sizeof(thread_client_id) - 1);
350   socket_t thread_socket = client->socket;
351   bool is_webrtc = (thread_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
352   (void)is_webrtc; // May be unused in release builds
353 
354   log_debug("Video render thread: client_id=%s, webrtc=%d", thread_client_id, is_webrtc);
355 
356   log_info("[VIDEO_RENDER_THREAD_START] ★★★ LOCK STATE at thread entry for client %u", thread_client_id);
357   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash
358 
359   // Wait for client to send terminal capabilities before rendering
360   // Without capabilities, convert_composite_to_ascii() will fail
361   int timeout_ms = 5000; // 5 second timeout
362   int waited_ms = 0;
363   while (!client->has_terminal_caps && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
364     if (waited_ms == 0) {
365       log_debug("Waiting for terminal capabilities from client %u...", thread_client_id);
366     }
367     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
368     platform_sleep_ms(10);
369     waited_ms += 10;
370     if (waited_ms >= timeout_ms) {
371       log_warn("Timeout waiting for terminal capabilities from client %u (waited %dms)", thread_client_id, waited_ms);
372       return NULL; // Exit thread if capabilities never arrive
373     }
374   }
375   if (client->has_terminal_caps) {
376     log_debug("Received terminal capabilities for client %u after %dms", thread_client_id, waited_ms);
377   }
378 
379   // Get client's desired FPS from capabilities or use default
380   int client_fps = VIDEO_RENDER_FPS; // Default to 60 FPS
381   // Use snapshot pattern to avoid mutex in render thread
382   bool has_caps = client->has_terminal_caps;
383   int desired_fps = has_caps ? client->terminal_caps.desired_fps : 0;
384   if (has_caps && desired_fps > 0) {
385     client_fps = desired_fps;
386     log_debug("Client %u requested FPS: %d (has_caps=%d, desired_fps=%d)", thread_client_id, client_fps, has_caps,
387               desired_fps);
388   } else {
389     log_debug("Client %u using default FPS: %d (has_caps=%d, desired_fps=%d)", thread_client_id, client_fps, has_caps,
390               desired_fps);
391   }
392 
393   int base_frame_interval_ms = 1000 / client_fps;
394   log_debug("Client %u render interval: %dms (%d FPS)", thread_client_id, base_frame_interval_ms, client_fps);
395 
396   // FPS tracking for video render thread
397   fps_t video_fps_tracker = {0};
398   fps_init(&video_fps_tracker, client_fps, "SERVER VIDEO");
399 
400   // Adaptive sleep for frame rate limiting
401   adaptive_sleep_state_t sleep_state = {0};
402   adaptive_sleep_config_t config = {
403       .baseline_sleep_ns = (uint64_t)(NS_PER_SEC_INT / client_fps), // Dynamic FPS (typically 16.67ms for 60 FPS)
404       .min_speed_multiplier = 1.0,                                  // Constant rate (no slowdown)
405       .max_speed_multiplier = 1.0,                                  // Constant rate (no speedup)
406       .speedup_rate = 0.0,                                          // No adaptive behavior (constant FPS)
407       .slowdown_rate = 0.0                                          // No adaptive behavior (constant FPS)
408   };
409   adaptive_sleep_init(&sleep_state, &config);
410 
411   log_info("Video render loop STARTING for client %u", thread_client_id);
412 
413   bool should_continue = true;
414   while (should_continue && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
415     START_TIMER("render_iteration");
416     log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Video render loop iteration for client %u", thread_client_id);
417 
418     // Check for immediate shutdown
419     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
420       log_debug("Video render thread stopping for client %u (g_should_exit)", thread_client_id);
421       break;
422     }
423 
424     bool video_running = atomic_load_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running);
425     bool active = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);
426     bool shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down);
427 
428     should_continue = video_running && active && !shutting_down;
429 
430     if (!should_continue) {
431       log_debug("Video render thread stopping for client %u (should_continue=false: video_running=%d, active=%d, "
432                 "shutting_down=%d)",
433                 thread_client_id, video_running, active, shutting_down);
434       break;
435     }
436 
437     // Frame rate limiting using adaptive sleep system
438     // Use queue_depth=0 and target_depth=0 for constant-rate renderer (no backlog management)
439     uint64_t iter_start_ns = time_get_ns();
440     START_TIMER("render_adaptive_sleep");
441     adaptive_sleep_do(&sleep_state, 0, 0);
442     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_adaptive_sleep", "adaptive_sleep completed");
443     uint64_t after_sleep_ns = time_get_ns();
444 
445     // Capture timestamp for FPS tracking and frame timestamps
446     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
447 
448     // Check thread state again before acquiring locks (client might have been destroyed during sleep).
449     should_continue = atomic_load_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active) &&
450                       !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down);
451     if (!should_continue) {
452       break;
453     }
454 
455     // Optimization: No mutex needed - all fields are atomic or stable.
456     // client_id: stable string pointer
457     // width/height: stable uint16_t values (set once, never modified)
458     // active: atomic_t - use atomic_load for thread safety
459     const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;            // Stable read
460     unsigned short width_snapshot = client->width;                 // Stable uint16_t
461     unsigned short height_snapshot = client->height;               // Stable uint16_t
462     bool active_snapshot = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);      // Atomic read
463 
464     // Check if client is still active after getting snapshot
465     if (!active_snapshot) {
466       break;
467     }
468 
469     // Phase 2 IMPLEMENTED: Generate frame specifically for THIS client using snapshot data
470     size_t frame_size = 0;
471 
472     // Check if any clients are sending video
473     bool has_video_sources = any_clients_sending_video();
474 
475     // DIAGNOSTIC: Track when video sources become available
476     static bool last_has_sources = false;
477     if (has_video_sources != last_has_sources) {
478       log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u video sources: %s", thread_client_id,
479                has_video_sources ? "AVAILABLE" : "UNAVAILABLE");
480       last_has_sources = has_video_sources;
481     }
482 
483     log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT,
484                     "Video render iteration for client %u: has_video_sources=%d, width=%u, height=%u", thread_client_id,
485                     has_video_sources, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
486 
487     // Use default dimensions if client dimensions not received
488     if (width_snapshot == 0 || height_snapshot == 0) {
489       log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Using default dimensions for client %u (client reported: width=%u, height=%u)",
490                     thread_client_id, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
491       // Use 80x25 as standard terminal size fallback for snapshot/test clients
492       width_snapshot = 80;
493       height_snapshot = 25;
494     }
495 
496     // Generate frames for all clients with valid dimensions, regardless of whether other clients are sending video.
497     // This ensures WebSocket snapshot clients (receive-only mode) receive frames even when no other clients are
498     // sending video.
499     int sources_count = 0; // Track number of video sources in this frame
500 
501     // DIAGNOSTIC: Track every frame generation attempt
502     // Use per-client static variables to track frequency independently
503     static uint32_t frame_gen_count = 0;
504     static uint64_t frame_gen_start_time = 0;
505 
506     frame_gen_count++;
507     if (frame_gen_count == 1) {
508       frame_gen_start_time = current_time_ns;
509     }
510 
511     // Log every 120 attempts (should be ~2 seconds at 60 Hz)
512     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
513       uint64_t elapsed_ns = current_time_ns - frame_gen_start_time;
514       double gen_fps = (120.0 / (elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT));
515       log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u LOOP running at %.1f FPS (120 iterations in %.2fs)", thread_client_id, gen_fps,
516                elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT);
517     }
518 
519     log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "About to call create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client for client %u with dims %ux%u",
520                   thread_client_id, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
521 
522     // debug_sync_print_state() is too expensive to call every frame (kills FPS)
523 
524     uint64_t frame_create_start_ns = time_get_ns();
525     START_TIMER("render_create_frame");
526     char *ascii_frame = create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client(client_id_snapshot, width_snapshot, height_snapshot, false,
527                                                             &frame_size, NULL, &sources_count);
528     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_create_frame", "create_mixed_ascii_frame completed");
529     uint64_t frame_create_end_ns = time_get_ns();
530 
531     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
532       char sleep_str[32], create_str[32];
533       time_pretty((uint64_t)(after_sleep_ns - iter_start_ns), -1, sleep_str, sizeof(sleep_str));
534       time_pretty((uint64_t)(frame_create_end_ns - frame_create_start_ns), -1, create_str, sizeof(create_str));
535       log_warn("  TIMING: adaptive_sleep=%s, frame_create=%s", sleep_str, create_str);
536     }
537 
538     // Always send frames at configured FPS, even if no external video sources yet
539     // This ensures single-client mode (like testing) still gets continuous frames
540     // The frame data (animated background or test pattern) is still valid content
541 
542     // DEBUG: Log frame generation details
543     static uint32_t last_frame_hash = -1; // Initialize to -1 so first frame is always new
544     uint32_t current_frame_hash = 0;
545     if (ascii_frame && frame_size > 0) {
546       for (size_t i = 0; i < frame_size && i < 1000; i++) {
547         current_frame_hash = (uint32_t)((uint64_t)current_frame_hash * 31 + ((unsigned char *)ascii_frame)[i]);
548       }
549       if (current_frame_hash != last_frame_hash) {
550         log_info("RENDER_FRAME CHANGE: Client %u frame #%zu sources=%d hash=0x%08x (prev=0x%08x)", thread_client_id,
551                  frame_size, sources_count, current_frame_hash, last_frame_hash);
552         last_frame_hash = current_frame_hash;
553       } else {
554         log_dev_every(25000, "RENDER_FRAME DUPLICATE: Client %u frame #%zu sources=%d hash=0x%08x (no change)",
555                       thread_client_id, frame_size, sources_count, current_frame_hash);
556       }
557     }
558 
559     log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT,
560                   "create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client returned: ascii_frame=%p, frame_size=%zu, sources_count=%d",
561                   (void *)ascii_frame, frame_size, sources_count);
562 
563     // Phase 2 IMPLEMENTED: Write frame to double buffer (never drops!)
564     if (ascii_frame && frame_size > 0) {
565       log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Buffering frame for client %u (size=%zu)", thread_client_id, frame_size);
566       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE DETECTION: Store source count with frame
567       // Send thread will compare this with last sent count to detect grid changes
568       atomic_store_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, sources_count);
569 
570       // Use double-buffer system which has its own internal swap_mutex
571       // No external locking needed - the double-buffer is thread-safe by design
572       video_frame_buffer_t *vfb_snapshot = client->outgoing_video_buffer;
573 
574       if (vfb_snapshot) {
575         video_frame_t *write_frame = video_frame_begin_write(vfb_snapshot);
576         if (write_frame) {
577           // Copy ASCII frame data to the back buffer (NOT holding rwlock - just double-buffer's internal lock)
578           if (write_frame->data && frame_size <= vfb_snapshot->allocated_buffer_size) {
579             memcpy(write_frame->data, ascii_frame, frame_size);
580             write_frame->size = frame_size;
581             write_frame->capture_timestamp_ns = current_time_ns;
582 
583             // Always commit frame at configured FPS rate (adaptive_sleep_do enforces timing).
584             // Never skip frames based on content changes.
585             {
586               uint64_t commit_start_ns = time_get_ns();
587               // Commit the frame (swaps buffers atomically using vfb->swap_mutex, NOT rwlock)
588               START_TIMER("render_video_frame_commit");
589               video_frame_commit(vfb_snapshot);
590               STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_video_frame_commit", "video_frame_commit completed");
591               uint64_t commit_end_ns = time_get_ns();
592               char commit_duration_str[32];
593               time_pretty((uint64_t)(commit_end_ns - commit_start_ns), -1, commit_duration_str,
594                           sizeof(commit_duration_str));
595 
596               static uint32_t commits_count = 0;
597               static uint64_t commits_start_time = 0;
598               commits_count++;
599               if (commits_count == 1) {
600                 commits_start_time = commit_end_ns;
601               }
602               if (commits_count % 10 == 0) {
603                 uint64_t elapsed_ns = commit_end_ns - commits_start_time;
604                 double commit_fps = (10.0 / (elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT));
605                 log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u UNIQUE frames being sent at %.1f FPS (10 commits counted)",
606                          thread_client_id, commit_fps);
607               }
608 
609               log_info("[FRAME_COMMIT_TIMING] Client %u frame commit took %s (hash=0x%08x)", thread_client_id,
610                        commit_duration_str, current_frame_hash);
611 
612               // ARCHITECTURE: Frame transmission is handled EXCLUSIVELY by the send thread
613               // The send thread reads from this buffer and applies proper 60 FPS rate limiting
614               // Do NOT send from render thread - it bypasses rate limiting and causes network congestion
615               // Render thread responsibility: Generate frame → Write to buffer → Done
616               // Send thread responsibility: Read from buffer → Apply 60 FPS pacing → Transmit
617             }
618 
619           } else {
620             log_warn("Frame too large for buffer: %zu > %zu", frame_size, vfb_snapshot->allocated_buffer_size);
621           }
622 
623           // FPS tracking - frame successfully generated (handles lag detection and periodic reporting)
624           fps_frame_ns(&video_fps_tracker, current_time_ns, "frame rendered");
625         }
626       }
627 
628       SAFE_FREE(ascii_frame);
629     } else {
630       // No frame generated (probably no video sources) - this is normal, no error logging needed
631       log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Per-client render: No video sources available for client %u",
632                     client_id_snapshot);
633     }
634 
635     uint64_t iter_end_ns = time_get_ns();
636     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
637       char total_str[32];
638       time_pretty((uint64_t)(iter_end_ns - iter_start_ns), -1, total_str, sizeof(total_str));
639       log_warn("  TIMING: TOTAL_ITERATION=%s", total_str);
640     }
641     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_iteration", "render_iteration completed (total)");
642   }
643 
644 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
645   log_debug("Video render thread stopped for client %u", thread_client_id);
646 #endif
647 
648   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
649   asciichat_errno_destroy();
650 
651   return NULL;
652 }






References client_info::active, adaptive_sleep_do(),
    adaptive_sleep_init(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size,
    any_clients_sending_video(), APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), video_frame_t::capture_timestamp_ns,
    client_info::client_id, create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client(),
    video_frame_t::data, terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps,
    fps_frame_ns(), fps_init(), g_should_exit,
    client_info::has_terminal_caps, client_info::height,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN, NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT,
    client_info::outgoing_video_buffer, platform_sleep_ms(),
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_STRNCPY, client_info::shutting_down,
    video_frame_t::size, client_info::socket, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG, client_info::terminal_caps,
    time_get_ns(), time_pretty(),
    video_frame_begin_write(), video_frame_commit(),
    VIDEO_RENDER_FPS, client_info::video_render_thread_running,
    and client_info::width.


Referenced by create_client_render_threads().




int
  create_client_render_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx,
  client_info_t * client)


Create and initialize per-client rendering threads. Sets up the
    complete per-client threading infrastructure including both video and audio
    rendering threads plus all necessary synchronization primitives. This
    function is called once per client during connection establishment.










INITIALIZATION
  SEQUENCE: 



  
1.

  
MUTEX INITIALIZATION:

  
 •

  
client_state_mutex: Protects client state variables

  
2.

  
THREAD CREATION:

  
 •

  
Create video rendering thread (60fps)

  
 •

  
Create audio rendering thread (172fps)

  
 •

  
Set running flags with proper synchronization

  
3.

  
ERROR HANDLING:

  
 •

  
Complete cleanup if any step fails

  
 •

  
Prevent partially initialized client state

  
 •

  
Ensure no resource leaks on failure








THREAD
  SYNCHRONIZATION SETUP: 


PER-CLIENT MUTEXES: Each client gets dedicated mutexes for
    fine-grained locking:


    
  
  • Prevents contention between clients
    • 
  
  • Allows concurrent processing of multiple clients
    • 
  
  • Enables lock-free operations within client context
    • 



THREAD RUNNING FLAGS:


    
  
  • Protected by client_state_mutex
    • 
  
  • Allow graceful thread termination
    • 
  
  • Checked frequently by render threads
    • 







ERROR
  RECOVERY: 


PARTIAL FAILURE HANDLING: If thread creation fails partway
    through:


    
  
  • Stop and join any already-created threads
    • 
  
  • Destroy any initialized mutexes
    • 
  
  • Return error code to caller
    • 
  
  • Leave client in clean state for retry or cleanup
    • 



RESOURCE LEAK PREVENTION:


    
  
  • All allocations have corresponding cleanup
    • 
  
  • Thread handles are properly managed
    • 
  
  • Mutex destruction is deterministic
    • 







INTEGRATION
  POINTS: 


    
  
  • Called by add_client() in client.c
    • 
  
  • Threads integrate with stream.c and mixer.c
    • 
  
  • Cleanup handled by stop_client_render_threads()
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for thread creation


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Note


Function performs complete initialization or complete
  cleanup



Mutexes must be destroyed by
  stop_client_render_threads()




Thread handles must be joined before client destruction




Warning


Partial initialization is not supported - complete
  success or failure


See also


stop_client_render_threads() For cleanup
  implementation


Definition at line 1201 of file server/render.c.


1201                                                                                       {
1202   log_info("★★★ create_client_render_threads() CALLED for client_id=%s", client ? client->client_id : "NULL");
1203 
1204   if (!server_ctx || !client) {
1205     log_error("Cannot create render threads: NULL %s", !server_ctx ? "server_ctx" : "client");
1206     return -1;
1207   }
1208 
1209 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1210   log_debug("Creating render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1211 #endif
1212 
1213   // NOTE: Mutexes are already initialized in add_client() before any threads start
1214   // This prevents race conditions where receive thread tries to use uninitialized mutexes
1215 
1216   // Initialize render thread control flags
1217   // IMPORTANT: Set to true BEFORE creating thread to avoid race condition
1218   // where thread starts and immediately exits because flag is false
1219   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, true);
1220   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, true);
1221 
1222   // Create video rendering thread (stop_id=2, stop after receive thread)
1223   char thread_name[64];
1224   safe_snprintf(thread_name, sizeof(thread_name), "video_render_%s", client->client_id);
1225   asciichat_error_t video_result;
1226 
1227   if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1228     video_result = tcp_server_spawn_thread(server_ctx->tcp_server, client->socket, client_video_render_thread, client,
1229                                            2, thread_name);
1230   } else {
1231     log_debug("THREAD_CREATE: WebRTC/WebSocket render video thread for client %s", client->client_id);
1232     video_result =
1233         asciichat_thread_create(&client->video_render_thread, thread_name, client_video_render_thread, client);
1234   }
1235 
1236   if (video_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1237     // Reset flag since thread creation failed
1238     atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1239     // Mutexes will be destroyed by remove_client() which called us
1240     return -1;
1241   }
1242 
1243   // Create audio rendering thread (stop_id=2, same priority as video)
1244   safe_snprintf(thread_name, sizeof(thread_name), "audio_render_%s", client->client_id);
1245   asciichat_error_t audio_result;
1246 
1247   if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1248     audio_result = tcp_server_spawn_thread(server_ctx->tcp_server, client->socket, client_audio_render_thread, client,
1249                                            2, thread_name);
1250   } else {
1251     log_debug("THREAD_CREATE: WebRTC/WebSocket render audio thread for client %s", client->client_id);
1252     audio_result =
1253         asciichat_thread_create(&client->audio_render_thread, thread_name, client_audio_render_thread, client);
1254   }
1255   if (audio_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1256     // Clean up video thread (atomic operation, no mutex needed)
1257     atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1258     // Reset audio flag since thread creation failed
1259     atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1260     // tcp_server_stop_client_threads() will be called by remove_client()
1261     // to clean up the video thread we just created
1262     // Mutexes will be destroyed by remove_client() which called us
1263     return -1;
1264   }
1265 
1266 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1267   log_debug("Created render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1268 #endif
1269 
1270   return 0;
1271 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_create,
    atomic_store_bool(), client_info::audio_render_thread,
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    client_audio_render_thread(), client_info::client_id,
    client_video_render_thread(), client_info::is_tcp_client,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, safe_snprintf(),
    client_info::socket, server_context_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_spawn_thread(), client_info::video_render_thread,
    and client_info::video_render_thread_running.




void
  stop_client_render_threads (client_info_t * client)


Stop and cleanup per-client rendering threads. Performs graceful
    shutdown of both video and audio rendering threads for a specific client,
    including proper thread joining and resource cleanup. This function ensures
    deterministic cleanup without resource leaks.










SHUTDOWN
  SEQUENCE: 



  
1.

  
SIGNAL SHUTDOWN:

  
 •

  
Set thread running flags to false

  
 •

  
Threads detect flag change and exit processing loops

  
 •

  
Use mutex protection for atomic flag updates

  
2.

  
THREAD JOINING:

  
 •

  
Wait for video render thread to complete

  
 •

  
Wait for audio render thread to complete

  
 •

  
Handle join failures appropriately

  
3.

  
RESOURCE CLEANUP:

  
 •

  
Destroy per-client mutexes

  
 •

  
Clear thread handles

  
 •

  
Reset client thread state








GRACEFUL
  TERMINATION: 


THREAD COORDINATION:


    
  
  • Threads monitor running flags in their main loops
    • 
  
  • Flags are checked frequently (every iteration)
    • 
  
  • Threads exit cleanly within one processing cycle
    • 
  
  • No forced thread termination (unsafe)
    • 



DETERMINISTIC CLEANUP:


    
  
  • asciichat_thread_join() waits for complete thread exit
    • 
  
  • All thread resources are properly released
    • 
  
  • No zombie threads or resource leaks
    • 







ERROR
  HANDLING: 


THREAD JOIN FAILURES:


    
  
  • Logged as errors but don't prevent cleanup
    • 
  
  • Continue with resource cleanup regardless
    • 
  
  • Platform-specific error reporting
    • 



NULL CLIENT HANDLING:


    
  
  • Safe to call with NULL client pointer
    • 
  
  • Error logged and function returns safely
    • 
  
  • No undefined behavior or crashes
    • 







RESOURCE
  MANAGEMENT: 


MUTEX CLEANUP:


    
  
  • Destroys all per-client mutexes
    • 
  
  • Prevents resource leaks on client disconnect
    • 
  
  • Platform-independent destruction
    • 



THREAD HANDLE MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Clears thread handles after joining
    • 
  
  • Prevents accidental reuse of stale handles
    • 
  
  • Memory zeroing for safety
    • 







INTEGRATION
  REQUIREMENTS: 


    
  
  • Called by remove_client() in client.c
    • 
  
  • Must be called before freeing client structure
    • 
  
  • Coordinates with other cleanup functions
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for thread cleanup


Note


Function is safe to call multiple times (idempotent)



All resources are cleaned up regardless of errors




Thread joining may take up to one processing cycle




Warning


Must be called before client structure deallocation


See also


create_client_render_threads() For thread creation



asciichat_thread_join() For platform-specific joining




Definition at line 1354 of file server/render.c.


1354                                                        {
1355   if (!client) {
1356     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
1357     return;
1358   }
1359 
1360   log_debug("Stopping render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1361 
1362   // Signal threads to stop (atomic operations, no mutex needed)
1363   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1364   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1365 
1366   // Wait for threads to finish (deterministic cleanup)
1367   // During shutdown, don't wait forever for threads to join
1368   bool is_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
1369 
1370   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->video_render_thread)) {
1371     log_debug("Joining video render thread for client %u", client->client_id);
1372     int result;
1373     if (is_shutting_down) {
1374       // During shutdown, don't timeout - wait for thread to exit
1375       // Timeouts mask the real problem: threads that are still running
1376       log_debug("Shutdown mode: joining video render thread for client %u (no timeout)", client->client_id);
1377       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->video_render_thread, NULL);
1378       if (result != 0) {
1379         log_warn("Video render thread for client %u failed to join during shutdown: %s", client->client_id,
1380                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1381       }
1382     } else {
1383       log_debug("Calling asciichat_thread_join for video thread of client %u", client->client_id);
1384       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->video_render_thread, NULL);
1385       log_debug("asciichat_thread_join returned %d for video thread of client %u", result, client->client_id);
1386     }
1387 
1388     if (result == 0) {
1389 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1390       log_debug("Video render thread joined for client %u", client->client_id);
1391 #endif
1392     } else if (result != -2) { // Don't log timeout errors again
1393       if (is_shutting_down) {
1394         log_warn("Failed to join video render thread for client %u during shutdown (continuing): %s", client->client_id,
1395                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1396       } else {
1397         log_error("Failed to join video render thread for client %u: %s", client->client_id, SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1398       }
1399     }
1400     // Clear thread handle safely using platform abstraction
1401     asciichat_thread_init(&client->video_render_thread);
1402   }
1403 
1404   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->audio_render_thread)) {
1405     int result;
1406     if (is_shutting_down) {
1407       // During shutdown, don't timeout - wait for thread to exit
1408       // Timeouts mask the real problem: threads that are still running
1409       log_debug("Shutdown mode: joining audio render thread for client %u (no timeout)", client->client_id);
1410       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->audio_render_thread, NULL);
1411       if (result != 0) {
1412         log_warn("Audio render thread for client %u failed to join during shutdown: %s", client->client_id,
1413                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1414       }
1415     } else {
1416       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->audio_render_thread, NULL);
1417     }
1418 
1419     if (result == 0) {
1420 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1421       log_debug("Audio render thread joined for client %u", client->client_id);
1422 #endif
1423     } else if (result != -2) { // Don't log timeout errors again
1424       if (is_shutting_down) {
1425         log_warn("Failed to join audio render thread for client %u during shutdown (continuing): %s", client->client_id,
1426                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1427       } else {
1428         log_error("Failed to join audio render thread for client %u: %s", client->client_id, SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1429       }
1430     }
1431     // Clear thread handle safely using platform abstraction
1432     asciichat_thread_init(&client->audio_render_thread);
1433   }
1434 
1435   // DO NOT destroy the mutex here - client.c will handle it
1436   // mutex_destroy(&client->client_state_mutex);
1437 
1438 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1439   log_debug("Successfully destroyed render threads for client %u", client->client_id);
1440 #endif
1441 }






References asciichat_thread_init(),
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(), asciichat_thread_join,
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    client_info::audio_render_thread,
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    client_info::client_id, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    g_should_exit, log_debug, log_error, log_warn,
    SAFE_STRERROR, SET_ERRNO,
    client_info::video_render_thread, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.






Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
